---
title: 'Backup & Maintenance'
description: 'Database backup strategies, maintenance operations, and migration techniques for AmoraDB'
icon: 'hard-drive'
---

## Overview

Proper backup and maintenance procedures ensure data integrity and optimal performance. AmoraDB provides built-in methods for backup, optimization, and database management.

## Backup Operations

### backup() Method

Create a complete backup of your database:

```javascript
// Create backup with auto-generated path
const backupPath = await db.backup();
console.log(`Backup created at: ${backupPath}`);
// Output: Backup created at: ./data/mydb_backup_1710504000000

// Create backup with custom path
const customBackup = await db.backup('/backups/mydb-2024-03-15');
console.log(`Backup saved to: ${customBackup}`);
```

The `backup()` method:
- Copies all table data files (`.jsonl`)
- Copies all metadata files (`.meta.json`)
- Copies database metadata (`_metadata.json`)
- Creates backup directory if it doesn't exist

<Tip>
Backups are full copies, not incremental. Each backup contains all database files.
</Tip>

### Automated Backup Schedule

```javascript
const cron = require('node-cron');

// Daily backup at 2 AM
cron.schedule('0 2 * * *', async () => {
  try {
    const backupPath = await db.backup();
    console.log(`Daily backup completed: ${backupPath}`);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Backup failed:', error.message);
    // Send alert notification
  }
});

// Weekly backup every Sunday at 3 AM
cron.schedule('0 3 * * 0', async () => {
  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
  await db.backup(`/weekly-backups/db-${timestamp}`);
});
```

### Backup Before Critical Operations

```javascript
async function performCriticalUpdate() {
  // Create backup before risky operation
  const backupPath = await db.backup();
  console.log('Backup created:', backupPath);
  
  try {
    // Perform critical updates
    await db.table('users').updateMany(
      { status: 'legacy' },
      { status: 'migrated', migratedAt: new Date().toISOString() }
    );
    
    console.log('Update successful');
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Update failed:', error.message);
    console.log('Restore from backup:', backupPath);
    throw error;
  }
}
```

### Backup Rotation Strategy

```javascript
const fs = require('fs').promises;
const path = require('path');

async function rotateBackups(maxBackups = 7) {
  const backupDir = './backups';
  
  // Create new backup
  const newBackup = await db.backup();
  
  // Get all backups sorted by date
  const files = await fs.readdir(backupDir);
  const backups = files
    .filter(f => f.startsWith('mydb_backup_'))
    .map(f => ({
      name: f,
      path: path.join(backupDir, f),
      timestamp: parseInt(f.split('_').pop())
    }))
    .sort((a, b) => b.timestamp - a.timestamp);
  
  // Remove old backups
  for (let i = maxBackups; i < backups.length; i++) {
    await fs.rm(backups[i].path, { recursive: true });
    console.log(`Removed old backup: ${backups[i].name}`);
  }
  
  return newBackup;
}

// Keep last 7 backups
await rotateBackups(7);
```

## Restore from Backup

To restore a backup, simply point AmoraDB to the backup directory:

```javascript
// Close current database
await db.close();

// Create new instance pointing to backup
const restoredDb = new AmoraDB('mydb', {
  dataPath: '/backups/mydb_backup_1710504000000'
});

await restoredDb.init();
console.log('Database restored from backup');

// Verify data
const count = await restoredDb.table('users').count();
console.log(`Restored ${count} users`);
```

<Steps>
  <Step title="Stop application">
    Ensure no processes are writing to the database
  </Step>
  
  <Step title="Copy backup files">
    Copy backup directory to your data location
  </Step>
  
  <Step title="Initialize database">
    Create new AmoraDB instance with backup path
  </Step>
  
  <Step title="Verify data">
    Check record counts and data integrity
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Database Maintenance

### flush() - Force Write to Disk

Force all pending writes to be persisted:

```javascript
// Insert records
for (let i = 0; i < 500; i++) {
  db.table('logs').insert({ message: `Log ${i}` });
}

// Force immediate persistence
await db.table('logs').flush();
console.log('All writes committed to disk');
```

**When to use `flush()`:**
- Before application shutdown
- After critical operations
- Before creating backups
- When ensuring data durability

### optimize() - Rebuild Indices

Optimize database performance by rebuilding all indices:

```javascript
// Optimize entire database
await db.optimize();
console.log('Database optimized');
```

The `optimize()` method:
1. Flushes all pending writes
2. Drops all existing indices
3. Rebuilds indices from current data
4. Compacts data files

<Warning>
Optimization can take several minutes for large databases. Run during maintenance windows.
</Warning>

### Table-Level Optimization

```javascript
// Optimize a single table
const table = db.table('users');

// Flush pending writes
await table.flush();

// Rebuild indices
const indices = table.indexManager.getIndices();
for (const field of indices) {
  table.dropIndex(field);
  await table.createIndex(field);
}

// Force compaction
await table.save();

console.log('Table optimized');
```

## Data Management

### truncate() - Clear Table Data

Remove all records from a table while keeping the table structure:

```javascript
// Clear all data from table
await db.table('logs').truncate();
console.log('Logs table cleared');

// Verify empty
const count = await db.table('logs').count();
console.log(`Records remaining: ${count}`); // 0
```

**What `truncate()` does:**
- Deletes all records
- Clears all indices
- Resets metadata
- Removes data file
- Keeps table structure

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="truncate()">
    ```javascript
    // Fast: Deletes file
    await db.table('logs').truncate();
    
    // Table still exists
    const table = db.table('logs');
    // Can immediately insert new data
    ```
  </Tab>
  
  <Tab title="deleteMany()">
    ```javascript
    // Slower: Marks records as deleted
    await db.table('logs').deleteMany({});
    
    // Requires compaction to reclaim space
    await db.table('logs').save();
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

### drop() - Delete Table

Permanently delete a table and all its data:

```javascript
// Drop a table
await db.table('old_logs').drop();

// Or use dropTable method
await db.dropTable('old_logs');

console.log('Table deleted');

// Table no longer exists
const tables = await db.listTables();
console.log('Remaining tables:', tables);
```

<Warning>
`drop()` and `truncate()` are irreversible. Always create a backup before deleting data.
</Warning>

### Dropping the Entire Database

```javascript
// Close database
await db.close();

// Delete all data
await db.drop();

console.log('Database completely removed');
```

## Migration Strategies

### Schema Migrations

AmoraDB is schemaless, but you may want to migrate data formats:

```javascript
async function migrateUserSchema() {
  console.log('Starting migration...');
  
  // Get all users
  const users = await db.table('users').all().execute();
  
  // Migrate each user
  for (const user of users) {
    // Add new field
    if (!user.fullName && user.firstName && user.lastName) {
      await db.table('users').update(user._id, {
        fullName: `${user.firstName} ${user.lastName}`
      });
    }
    
    // Rename field
    if (user.phone_number) {
      await db.table('users').update(user._id, {
        phone: user.phone_number,
        phone_number: undefined
      });
    }
    
    // Transform data
    if (typeof user.age === 'string') {
      await db.table('users').update(user._id, {
        age: parseInt(user.age, 10)
      });
    }
  }
  
  console.log(`Migrated ${users.length} users`);
}

await migrateUserSchema();
```

### Large-Scale Migrations

For large datasets, process in batches:

```javascript
async function batchMigration() {
  const batchSize = 1000;
  let skip = 0;
  let processed = 0;
  
  while (true) {
    // Get batch
    const batch = await db.table('users')
      .all()
      .skip(skip)
      .limit(batchSize)
      .execute();
    
    if (batch.length === 0) break;
    
    // Process batch
    for (const record of batch) {
      // Transform data
      const updates = {
        status: record.status || 'active',
        updatedAt: new Date().toISOString()
      };
      
      await db.table('users').update(record._id, updates);
      processed++;
    }
    
    console.log(`Processed ${processed} records...`);
    skip += batchSize;
    
    // Flush periodically
    if (processed % 5000 === 0) {
      await db.table('users').flush();
    }
  }
  
  console.log(`Migration complete: ${processed} records`);
}

await batchMigration();
```

### Version Control for Migrations

```javascript
const migrations = [
  {
    version: 1,
    name: 'add_fullName_field',
    up: async (db) => {
      const users = await db.table('users').all().execute();
      for (const user of users) {
        if (user.firstName && user.lastName) {
          await db.table('users').update(user._id, {
            fullName: `${user.firstName} ${user.lastName}`
          });
        }
      }
    }
  },
  {
    version: 2,
    name: 'normalize_phone_numbers',
    up: async (db) => {
      // Migration logic
    }
  }
];

async function migrate(db) {
  // Get current version from metadata
  const currentVersion = db.metadata.migrationVersion || 0;
  
  // Run pending migrations
  for (const migration of migrations) {
    if (migration.version > currentVersion) {
      console.log(`Running migration: ${migration.name}`);
      await migration.up(db);
      db.metadata.migrationVersion = migration.version;
      await db.saveMetadata();
      console.log(`Migration ${migration.version} complete`);
    }
  }
}

await migrate(db);
```

## Database Health Checks

```javascript
async function healthCheck() {
  const health = {
    status: 'ok',
    checks: [],
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
  };
  
  try {
    // Check database connection
    const stats = await db.getStats();
    health.checks.push({
      name: 'database_connection',
      status: 'ok',
      tables: stats.tables,
      records: stats.totalRecords
    });
    
    // Check each table
    for (const tableName of await db.listTables()) {
      const table = db.table(tableName);
      const count = await table.count();
      
      health.checks.push({
        name: `table_${tableName}`,
        status: 'ok',
        records: count,
        pendingWrites: table.pendingWrites.length,
        pendingUpdates: table.pendingUpdates.size
      });
    }
    
    // Check cache performance
    const cacheStats = stats.cacheStats;
    for (const [tableName, cache] of Object.entries(cacheStats)) {
      const hitRate = cache.hits / (cache.hits + cache.misses);
      health.checks.push({
        name: `cache_${tableName}`,
        status: hitRate > 0.5 ? 'ok' : 'warning',
        hitRate: hitRate.toFixed(2),
        message: hitRate < 0.5 ? 'Consider increasing cache size' : null
      });
    }
    
  } catch (error) {
    health.status = 'error';
    health.error = error.message;
  }
  
  return health;
}

const health = await healthCheck();
console.log(JSON.stringify(health, null, 2));
```

## Graceful Shutdown

Always close the database properly on application shutdown:

```javascript
// Handle shutdown signals
process.on('SIGINT', async () => {
  console.log('Shutting down gracefully...');
  
  try {
    // Flush all pending writes
    await db.close();
    console.log('Database closed successfully');
    process.exit(0);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error during shutdown:', error);
    process.exit(1);
  }
});

process.on('SIGTERM', async () => {
  console.log('SIGTERM received, closing database...');
  await db.close();
  process.exit(0);
});
```

The `close()` method:
- Flushes all pending writes
- Saves pending updates and deletes
- Saves database metadata
- Emits 'close' event

## Maintenance Checklist

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Daily" icon="calendar-day">
    - Monitor cache hit rates
    - Check pending operation counts
    - Review error logs
    - Automated backups
  </Card>
  
  <Card title="Weekly" icon="calendar-week">
    - Review database statistics
    - Check disk space usage
    - Verify backup integrity
    - Rotate old backups
  </Card>
  
  <Card title="Monthly" icon="calendar">
    - Run full optimization
    - Audit index usage
    - Review query performance
    - Test backup restoration
  </Card>
  
  <Card title="As Needed" icon="wrench">
    - Apply schema migrations
    - Tune compaction settings
    - Adjust cache sizes
    - Archive old data
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Best Practices

<Steps>
  <Step title="Regular backups">
    Automate daily backups and test restoration procedures
  </Step>
  
  <Step title="Monitor performance">
    Track cache hit rates and query performance
  </Step>
  
  <Step title="Graceful shutdown">
    Always call `close()` before application exit
  </Step>
  
  <Step title="Test migrations">
    Test migrations on a backup before running on production
  </Step>
  
  <Step title="Document procedures">
    Maintain runbooks for backup and recovery procedures
  </Step>
</Steps>
